description:
Line 337: bytes.to_vec() creates a plain Vec<u8> from the Zeroizing<Vec<u8>>. This plain Vec is passed into MaxNvBuffer::try_from() and is NOT zeroed on drop. The comment on lines 335-336 claims 'both copies are zeroed on drop' but this is incorrect — the intermediate Vec<u8> produced by .to_vec() is a standard allocation with no zeroize-on-drop guarantee.

Trigger: any call to put(). The secret bytes remain in freed heap memory until the allocator reuses the page.

Fix: use MaxNvBuffer::try_from(bytes.as_slice()) if that conversion exists, or convert from the Zeroizing<Vec<u8>> directly. If the API requires Vec<u8>, acknowledge the leak in the comment rather than claiming zeroed-on-drop.

Location: secretx-tpm2/src/lib.rs line 337.
title: put() leaks unzeroed copy of secret bytes
